titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( Smith ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( Smith ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1908. Only two messages from Dairen have been received in Moscow. One came through FonOff, was subject of my telegram 1325, April 25; second, through commercial channels, reported in Embtel 1563, May 18. I spoke to LozovskiSolomon Abramovich Lozovsky, Soviet Deputy Minister for Foreign Affairs. on June 9 (ReEmbtel 1850, June 10Not printed.) in regard communication facilities for Benninghoff at Dairen and I confirmed in letter to him on June 10 our request for arrangements to be made to provide communication facilities for Dairen, at same time requesting him to confirm receipt by Benninghoff of our message transmitted through FonOff on April 25. Lozovski says Moscow communications with Dairen are very sketchy and I am inclined to believe him.

This is in reply to Deptel 1975 [1075], June 12.Not printed; it repeated telegram No. 1, June 6, 5 p.m., from the Consul General at Dairen, supra. Dept please repeat to Nanking.Repeated in Department’s unnumbered telegram of June 18, noon.
